mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 24-month rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not statistically higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate equity sell-offs and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to predict direction with a t-statistic below 1.5 over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand recovery, driving sustained positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 60-day returns in over 60% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during late-cycle expansions reflects capacity utilization tightening and margin expansion, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The signal generates negative 20-day returns in late-cycle regimes, or the predictive coefficient for INDPRO turns negative when filtered for late-cycle macro conditions.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month CPI increase exceeding 0.3% forces markets to price in aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in more than 50% of high-inflation trigger months, or the signal information coefficient drops below zero in rolling 36-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, boosting corporate earnings expectations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal fails to predict positive returns in over 55% of cases, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant when tested against market volatility regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter GDP growth exceeding 2.0% during early-cycle expansions validates economic momentum, supporting equity risk premiums and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The GDP signal produces negative 60-day returns in early-cycle regimes, or the predictive relationship reverses when GDP growth is filtered for early-cycle macro conditions.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive months of declining industrial production indicate immediate supply-side contraction and weakening economic activity, triggering rapid equity deleveraging and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to achieve a statistically significant negative coefficient in event-study regressions.
